_meta.description: EEG sample registry — tagged datasets for BCI security research and testing
_meta.schema: Each entry: id, name, source, sourceUrl, type, condition[], dsm5Code, channels, samplingRateHz, format, license, redistributable, subjects, taraId, paradigm, notes

samples.name: ADHD Adult Resting State + Cognitive
samples.paradigm: Eyes open, eyes closed, cognitive challenge, sound listening
samples.notes: CC BY 4.0 — freely redistributable with attribution. Subject 7 female ADHD group has corrupted signal. Published by Nasrabadi et al.

samples.name: ADHD/Control Children EEG
samples.subjectBreakdown: 61 ADHD + 60 controls, ages 7-12, boys and girls
samples.notes: IEEE DataPort Open Access — requires IEEE account, no redistribution rights. DSM-IV diagnosed, Ritalin-treated up to 6 months.

samples.name: FOCUS: ADHD EEG During Gameplay
samples.subjectBreakdown: ADHD and non-ADHD adults during brain-controlled game
samples.paradigm: Active cognitive gameplay with EMOTIV Epoc
samples.notes: IEEE DataPort Open Access. DOI: 10.21227/0wa8-0b66. Alchalabi, Shirmohammadi, Eddin (2020).

samples.name: CHB-MIT Scalp EEG (Epilepsy)
samples.subjectBreakdown: 22 pediatric (23 cases), 5M ages 3-22, 17F ages 1.5-19. 664 EDF files, 198 seizure events.
samples.paradigm: Continuous monitoring, post-medication-withdrawal, seizure annotations in .seizures files
samples.notes: Children's Hospital Boston / MIT. ODC-BY — freely redistributable. Gold standard for seizure detection research.

samples.name: Sleep-EDF Database Expanded
samples.subjectBreakdown: SC: 153 healthy adults 25-101 (1987-91). ST: 44 recordings, 22 subjects, temazepam study (1994).
samples.paradigm: Whole-night PSG, sleep stage labels: W, N1, N2, N3, REM
samples.notes: ODC-BY — freely redistributable. Standard benchmark for sleep staging algorithms.

samples.name: PhysioNet EEG Motor Movement/Imagery
samples.paradigm: Real and imagined movement: left fist, right fist, both fists, both feet + baseline
samples.notes: ODC-BY — freely redistributable. Already integrated in qif-lab/src/real_data.py via download_eegbci().

samples.name: BCI Competition IV Dataset 2a (4-Class)
samples.paradigm: 4-class motor imagery: left hand, right hand, feet, tongue
samples.notes: Academic citation required. Available via MOABB as BNCI2014_001.

samples.name: UCI EEG Database (Alcoholism)
samples.subjectBreakdown: Alcoholic (genetic predisposition) vs. control, visual stimulus paradigm
samples.paradigm: Visual stimulus matching: 1-stimulus, match, no-match conditions
samples.notes: CC BY 4.0 — freely redistributable. Often confused with ADHD in secondary sources; this is alcoholism data.

samples.name: DEAP Emotion Dataset
samples.paradigm: Arousal, valence, dominance, liking — continuous self-report
samples.notes: Academic only — requires application with institutional email. Koelstra et al. IEEE TAFFC 2012.

samples.name: SEED Emotion EEG Dataset
samples.subjectBreakdown: 15 subjects x 3 sessions (~1 week apart), 15 film clips/session
samples.paradigm: Positive, negative, neutral emotion elicited by film stimuli
samples.notes: Academic registration required. Differential entropy features pre-computed. Variants: SEED-IV, SEED-V, SEED-VIG.

samples.name: QIF-Lab Healthy Baseline (Synthetic)
samples.paradigm: Mathematically synthesized multi-band EEG (delta, theta, alpha, beta, gamma)
samples.notes: QIF-internal synthetic. BrainFlow SYNTHETIC_BOARD or custom generator. 8 scenarios available: healthy_baseline, noisy_but_safe, degraded_signal, signal_injection_attack, phase_disruption_attack, amplitude_spike_attack, quantum_regime_short, decoherence_boundary.

samples.name: QIF-Lab BrainFlow Synthetic Board
samples.subjectBreakdown: Generated in real-time by BrainFlow SYNTHETIC_BOARD
samples.paradigm: Realistic multi-band spectral content, no clinical meaning
samples.notes: BrainFlow SDK synthetic board. No pre-recorded files — generates data on demand. Used in test_brainflow.py and synthetic_data.py.

samples.name: Neurowall SSVEP Attack Scenario
samples.paradigm: Clean EEG + injected SSVEP at 15Hz and 10.9Hz, processed through 3-layer Neurowall pipeline
samples.notes: QIF-internal. Tests L1 notch filter, L2 differential privacy, L3 NISS policy engine. Paired with neurowall ROC data (roc_data.json).

samples.name: Neurosim Signal Injection Attacks
samples.paradigm: Multiple TARA-mapped attack techniques: SSVEP injection, impedance manipulation, neuronal flooding, signal replay
samples.notes: QIF-internal. Full attack simulator with registry.py mapping to TARA catalog. Includes signal_injection.py, evasion.py, feedback.py.

samples.name: Neurosim Evasion Attacks
samples.paradigm: Slow-onset evasion techniques designed to bypass detection thresholds
samples.notes: QIF-internal. DC drift, boiling frog (gradual amplitude shift), envelope modulation.

samples.name: Neurosim Closed-Loop Cascade Attack
samples.paradigm: Closed-loop feedback attack exploiting BCI decode-stimulate cycle
samples.notes: QIF-internal. Most dangerous attack class — self-amplifying through neurofeedback loop.
